payload.answer: Committed: Brain DB is source of truth; Cognee is rebuildable projection; Slack is strict memory intake. Preserved as open: Telegram addition; cheapest model that saturates performance.
payload.decision: Commit architectural principles as memory guidance; preserve open questions as non-committal items.

entity_resolution.reason: No entity IDs were provided; retain only the named entities and principles without binding to external IDs.

payload.intent: Compile durable memory cards from the provided chat summary for Brain (personal memory system).

memory_cards.statement: Brain DB should be the source of truth for memory, and Cognee should be treated as a rebuildable semantic projection.
Slack should be a strict memory intake agent.
Should Telegram be added later as an additional memory intake channel?
Which model is cheapest while saturating performance (given the system’s needs)?

details: Created 4 memory cards: 2 principles (high confidence) and 2 open/optimization questions (medium confidence).
Preserved ambiguity by not committing answers to open questions.

repair_options: If entity IDs are later available for Brain DB/Cognee/Slack, map the existing entity names to those IDs.
When decisions are made about Telegram or model selection, replace the open_question/optimization_question cards with final decision cards and remove ambiguity.
